model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Michigan State is the slight pick because this looks like a very competitive Round of 32 game, but the Spartans appear to bring the steadier overall tournament profile and fewer health questions into it. Louisville’s full-season metrics are strong enough to make the Cardinals a very real threat, yet the recent concern around Mikel Brown Jr.’s back injury slightly reduces their margin for error against a disciplined opponent. In a close matchup, Michigan State feels like the safer bracket choice.

reasoningStep.title: Balanced Michigan State edge in a close matchup
reasoningStep.summary: Louisville has a strong top-20 caliber profile, but recent reporting around Mikel Brown Jr.’s health creates some uncertainty. With no similarly significant availability concern found for Michigan State, the Spartans get a narrow edge in what still projects as a close game.
evidence: Louisville rated No. 16 in Torvik and No. 18 in KenPom in the available ratings, with top-25 offense and defense.
Recent web reporting noted Louisville guard Mikel Brown Jr. missed the ACC tournament with a lower back injury, though he was expected back for March Madness.
A regional preview specifically cited Brown’s injury as a reason Louisville could be vulnerable in this matchup.
No comparably meaningful Michigan State injury concern surfaced in the available searches, giving the Spartans a small stability edge.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
